document.title: IEC 60601-1-2 Test Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Nemko Korea IEC 60601-1-2 EMC test report REP016219 for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG, and HAD1717VC.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory and technical evidence that the tested detector fulfilled IEC 60601-1-2 and EN 60601-1-2 EMC requirements for the tested sample.
keyPoints: Report issued 2024-06-21 by Nemko Korea Co., Ltd. under Nemko responsibility.
Test item is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with model references HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG, and HAD1717VC.

Testing covered emissions and immunity including conducted emission, radiated RF emission, harmonics, flicker, ESD, RF fields, EFT/burst, surges, conducted RF, magnetic fields, voltage dips, interruptions, and proximity magnetic fields.
Intended environment is professional healthcare facility environment.

evidence.claim: The ratings for the power supply box are AC 100-240 V, 50/60 Hz, 80 VA input and DC 12 V output.
evidence.evidenceText: Ratings : Power supply box INPUT : AC (100 - 240) V, (50 / 60) Hz, 80 VA OUTPUT : DC 12 V

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not for mammography or direct cardiac application.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Use

evidence.evidenceText: Intended ENVIRONMENT Professional healthcare facility environment

evidence.claim: The product was classified as portable for installation and use.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification of installation and use : Portable

evidence.claim: The test item was received on 2023-09-11 and tests were performed from 2023-10-24 to 2023-12-19.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of receipt of test item : 2023-09-11 Date (s) of performance of tests : 2023-10-24 to 2023-12-19

evidence.claim: The emissions verdict summary shows passing results for CISPR 11 terminal disturbance voltages and radiation disturbance.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.1 Terminal disturbance voltages CISPR 11:2015 +A1:2016 + A2:2019 P 7.1 Radiation disturbance CISPR 11:2015 +A1:2016 + A2:2019 P

evidence.claim: The verdict summary shows passing results for AC mains harmonics and voltage fluctuations/flicker.
evidence.evidenceText: 7.2.1 AC-Mains Harmonics IEC 61000-3-2:2018/AMD1:2020 P 7.2.2 AC-Mains Voltage fluctuations and flicker IEC 61000-3-3:2013/AMD1:2017 P

evidence.claim: The immunity verdict summary shows passing results for ESD, radiated RF EM fields, EFT/burst, surges, conducted RF disturbances, magnetic fields, voltage dips, voltage interruptions, RF wireless proximity fields, and proximity magnetic fields.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-2 referenced immunity standards Clause Requirement – Test case Basic standard Verdict 8.9 ELECTROSTATIC DISCHARGE IEC 61000-4-2:2008 P 8.9 Radiated RF EM fields IEC 61000-4-3:2020 P 8.9 Electrical fast transients / bursts IEC 61000-4-4:2012 P 8.9 Surges IEC 61000-4-5:2014+A1:2017 P 8.9 Conducted disturbances induced by RF fields IEC 61000-4-6:2023 P 8.9 RATED power frequency magnetic fields IEC 61000-4-8:2009 P 8.9 Voltage dips IEC 61000-4-11:2020 P 8.9 Voltage interruptions IEC 61000-4-11:2020 P 8.10 IMMUNITY to proximity fields from RF wireless communications equipment IEC 60601-1-2:2014/AMD1:2020 Table 9 P 8.11 Immunity to proximity magnetic fields IEC 61000-4-39:2017 P

evidence.claim: Essential performance criteria included normal operation, consistent DC power from the power box during EMC testing, normal operation of the manufacturer program when connected to the power box, and acquisition of intended images.
evidence.evidenceText: The essential performance for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector during EMC test was defined as follows. - Operation normally when operation is required - DC power is consistently supplied from Power box when EMC test is performed. - When connected to Power box, program provided by manufacturer is operated normally. - Acquisition image is obtained as intended.

evidence.claim: The report states that test results relate only to the object tested.
evidence.evidenceText: The test results presented in this report relate only to the object tested.

evidence.claim: The report is not valid as a CB Test Report unless signed by an approved IECEE Testing Laboratory and appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is not valid as a CB Test Report unless signed by an approved IECEE Testing Laboratory and appended to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB in accordance with IECEE 02.

reviewFlags.reason: The source is an IEC 60601-1-2 test report, not a certificate; CB Test Report validity depends on signature and attachment to a CB Test Certificate issued by an NCB.
Authority or jurisdiction is not a governmental regulator; the document identifies Nemko/Nemko Korea as testing laboratory authority.
Pages 60 through 101 contain page headers but no extracted substantive content in the provided text, suggesting omitted attachment data or low-value OCR extraction for those pages.
